# Loomly Tour

*A walkthrough of the Loomly dashboard, captured per scene by the visual editor.*

**URL:** http://localhost:8765/

## Contents

1. [Welcome back, Alex](#1-welcome-back-alex)
2. [Browse the project list](#2-browse-the-project-list)
3. [Open the new-project dialog](#3-open-the-new-project-dialog)

## 1. Welcome back, Alex <a id="1-welcome-back-alex"></a>

### 1.1 Land on the dashboard

This is your Loomly dashboard — a snapshot of every project in flight.

1. Notice **the stats row**.

> Three quick stats up top — active, due, shipped

## 2. Browse the project list <a id="2-browse-the-project-list"></a>

### 2.1 Scan the project list

Each row shows the brief, owner, status, and the next deadline.

1. Look at **the second project row**.
2. Notice **the at-risk project row**.

> Q3 launch plan is at risk — flagged in amber

## 3. Open the new-project dialog <a id="3-open-the-new-project-dialog"></a>

### 3.1 Open the new-project dialog

Click + New project to start a fresh one.

1. Notice **the new-project button**.
2. Click the target element. *(no description — line 70)*

## 4. Fill in and create a project <a id="4-fill-in-and-create-a-project"></a>

### 4.1 Type the project name

Type a name and a brief, then hit Create.

1. Click the target element. *(no description — line 82)*
2. Look at **the name input**.
3. Type **"Holiday landing page"**.

### 4.2 Type the description

1. Look at **the description input**.
2. Type **"Festive variant for the homepage hero"**.

### 4.3 Submit the form

The new row appears at the top.

1. Click the target element. *(no description — line 96)*
2. Notice **the newly-added project row**.

> And it's in the list

---

- line 70: click has no description (selector: [data-testid='new-project-btn'])
- line 82: click has no description (selector: [data-testid='new-project-btn'])
- line 96: click has no description (selector: [data-testid='create-btn'])
